rulings.Case Title: IN RE: TARA MATERIALS, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Order Correcting Or Vacating Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 14, 2019, Line 1, PETITIONER TARA MATERIALS, INC.'s Petition For Order Correcting Or Vacating Arbitration Award. (part 1 of 2 of tenetative ruling) Petitioner Tara Materials, Inc.'s Petition for Order Correcting or Vacating Arbitration Award is denied without prejudice. Tara may renew the motion once the arbitration panel issues a final award. CCP § 1283.4 provides: "The award shall be in writing and signed by the arbitrators concurring therein. It shall include a determination of all the questions submitted to the arbitrators the decision of which is necessary in order to determine the controversy." "Before confirming an award, the trial court has a duty, in order to follow the dictates of section 1283.4, to ensure that the arbitrator's 'award' is an 'award' within the meaning of that statute." (Cinel v. Christopher (2012) 203 Cal.App.4th 759, 767.) A partial final arbitration award is not a sufficient award, and a court lacks jurisdiction to confirm it. (Kaiser Foundation Health Plan, Inc. v. Superior Court (2017) 13 Cal.App.5th 1125, 1131, 1142-1144; see also Maplebear, Inc. v. Busick (2018) 26 Cal.App.5th 394, 402-407 [trial court correctly ruled it had no jurisdiction to review arbitrator's partial final award]; Judge v. Niijar Realty, Inc. (2014) 232 Cal.App.4th 619, 634 fn. 12.) In this case, the Court lacks jurisdiction to vacate or correct the "INTERIM AWARD." That interim award expressly reserves several key issues in the parties' dispute (the "Reserved Matters") for further resolution. For example, the arbitration panel must determine whether the parties are subject to a non-compete provision. The panel also has yet to rule upon the accounting and winding down of Fine Arts Express, LLC ("FAE"), the joint venture formed by the parties that is at the heart of their dispute. The cases Tara relies upon directly address only the arbitrator's authority to issue incremental or multistep awards, rather than the trial court's jurisdiction to review such awards, and in any event are distinguishable. For example, in Hightower v. Superior Court (2001) 86 Cal.App.4th 1415, the court held only that the arbitrator could properly resolve certain critical areas of the parties' dispute in a partial final award but reserve jurisdiction to later decide, by a final award, issues which will likely arise as a result of the implementation of that remedy. (Id. at 1419.) The incremental process structured by the arbitrator was "reasonably necessary, if not essential, to the effective establishment and enforcement of the remedy that the arbitrator has fashioned," which allowed one party to obtain financing to acquire the other's shares as a remedy for a breach of the shareholders agreement. (Id. at 1439.) The arbitrator had determined all of the issues that were necessary to resolve the dispute, and nothing remained to be resolved except "potential and conditional issues" that could not have been determined when the partial final award was issued. (Id.; see also EHM Productions, Inc. v. Starline Tours of Hollywood, Inc. (2018) 21 Cal.App.5th 1058, 1065-1066.) In Roehl v. Ritchie (2007) 147 Cal.App.4th 338, the interim award "effectively decided the most important dispute between the parties," i.e., whether a note was separate or community property. (Roehl, 147 Cal.App.4th at 340-341.) In Advantage Medical Services, LLC v Hoffman (2008) 160 Cal.App.4th 806, without addressing the jurisdictional issue, the court affirmed a trial court order vacating an interim award that had determined the merits of the dispute except for the amount of punitive damages. (part 1 of 2) =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: TARA MATERIALS, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner Tara Materials, Inc. Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Order Correcting Or Vacating Arbitration Award (Part 2 Of 2 For Tentative Ruling Only)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 14, 2019, Line 1, Petitioner Tara Materials, Inc.'s Petition For Order Correcting Or Vacating Arbitration Award (Part 2 Of 2 For Tentative Ruling Only). In this case, nothing in the interim award shows that the arbitration panel intended for that ruling to be reviewable or that immediate review is necessary to effectuate the arbitrators' remedy. One of the main issues in this dispute is the winding down of FAE, which has still not occurred. In other words, one of the most important disputes between the parties has yet to be fully adjudicated, and more than merely potential and conditional issues remain. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for ATI is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. (end of part 2 of 2)=(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: FARHAD ZAGHI VS. CECELIA KENNEDY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 14, 2019, Line 13, CROSS DEFENDANT STEWART TITLE OF CALIFORNIA, INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Cross-Defendant Stewart Title of California, Inc.'s motion to strike portions of Cecelia Kennedy's Third Amended Cross-Complaint is granted. The Third Amended Cross-Complaint violates the Court's February 20, 2019 Order re Stewart Title of California Inc.'s Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings, which stated in pertinent part, "Motion is granted without leave to amend as to causes of action for breach of fiduciary duty and negligence. No opposition filed. Motion is GRANTED with leave to amend to allege a breach of contract cause of action only against the moving defendant. No other amendments allowed. . . . All amendments to be in bold font." Within 5 days of this order, Plaintiff shall file and serve a Fourth Amended Cross-Complaint in strict conformity with the Court's prior order. Any "general allegations" shall be set out solely in the breach of contract cause of action. In addition, the Court grants the motion to strike the allegation in the prayer on pg. 21, line 21, "Punitive Damages from Cross-Defendant[] Stewart Title Company." As Ms. Kennedy concedes, punitive damages are not recoverable on a cause of action for breach of contract. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: FARHAD ZAGHI VS. CECELIA KENNEDY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 14, 2019, Line 13, CROSS DEFENDANT FARHAD ZAGHI's MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Plaintiff and Cross-Defendant Farhad Zaghi's motion to strike Defendant and Cross-Complainant Cecelia Kennedy's Third Amended Cross-Complaint is granted. The Third Amended Cross-Complaint violates the Court's February 20, 2019 Order re Stewart Title of California Inc.'s Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings, which stated in pertinent part, "Motion is granted without leave to amend as to causes of action for breach of fiduciary duty and negligence. No opposition filed. Motion is GRANTED with leave to amend to allege a breach of contract cause of action only against the moving defendant. No other amendments allowed. . . . All amendments to be in bold font." Within 5 days of this order, Plaintiff shall file and serve a Fourth Amended Cross-Complaint in strict conformity with the Court's prior order. Any "general allegations" shall be set out solely in the breach of contract cause of action. Ms. Zaghi's motion to strike the allegation in Paragraph 70 of the Third Amended Cross-Complaint that "Stewart Title has been paying Zaghi's attorneys' fees in this litigation" is denied without prejudice to the parties raising the issue in connection with the pending motions in limine. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Counsel for the plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES ROSENFIELD VS. THOMAS P. JACKOVICS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Thomas P. Jackovics Production Of Esi For Inspection And Copying Per Demand For Production Served In Connection With The Deposition Notice, And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, March 14, 2019, Line 2, PLAINTIFF JAMES ROSENFIELD'S Motion To Compel Defendant Thomas P. Jackovics Production Of Esi For Inspection And Copying Per Demand For Production Served In Connection With The Deposition Notice, And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Roger Mead, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing Required. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rmead@folgerlevin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: GRICELDA ROMERO ET AL VS. PAUL WONG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Initial Responses To Written Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, March 14, 2019, Line 3, DEFENDANT PAUL WONG'S Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Initial Responses To Written Discovery. Pro Tem Judge Roger Mead, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants' unopposed motion to compel initial responses to Defendants' Form Interrogatories (Set One), Special Interrogatories (Set One) and Demand For Production of Documents (Set One), and for monetary discovery sanctions in the amount of $90, is granted. Within 14 days of service of notice of entry of this order Plaintiffs shall provide code-compliant responses, without objections (including objections based on privilege or on the protection for work product, subject to the provisions of CCP §§ 2030.290(a) and 2031.300(a) providing for relief from waiver of such objections), and pay Defendants' counsel discovery sanctions in the requested amount. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rmead@folgerlevin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: EUGENE SORIANO VS. ARXAN TECHNOLOGIES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Cross Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 14, 2019, Line 10, DEFENDANT ARXAN TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'s Motion For Leave To File Cross Complaint. Defendant Arxan Technologies, Inc.'s motion for leave to file cross-complaint is granted. The liberal policy in favor of amendment and judicial economy support the motion. Plaintiff Eugene Soriano has not shown that he would be prejudiced by allowing the amendment or that Arxan has acted in "bad faith." Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/EPS)
